title: Apply backpressure, never unbounded buffers
description: When a client cannot keep up, the server bounds its send buffer and slows or drops, rather than growing memory.
body: Use when pushing a high-rate stream to clients of varying speed. Do not enqueue messages into an unbounded per-connection buffer when the socket's write is slow. Actions: bound each connection's outbound queue; when it fills, either apply backpressure to the producer, drop coalescible updates (keep latest state), or disconnect the laggard with a resync-on-reconnect contract. Validation signals include a slow-consumer test showing server memory stays flat and the slow client is shed or coalesced rather than buffered forever. Risk boundary: an unbounded send buffer lets one slow client exhaust server memory and take down all connections; escalate as a stability risk.
